headline: EV Week In Review: Musk Tapers Stake Sale, Apple Car Chatter Resurfaces, XPeng Unveils New SUV, Rivian, Lucid Caught Up In Irrational Exuberance And More
summary:
EV stocks turned in mixed performances for the week ending Nov. 19. EV makers swayed to stock-specific news, while charging stocks pulled back amid profit taking following a strong week of gains.

Here are the key events that happened in the EV space during the week:

headline: Female Worker Sues Elon Musk-Led Tesla For Rampant Sexual Harassment
summary: According to a lawsuit filed by a woman who works in Tesla's (NASDAQ: TSLA) Fremont, California factory, the company's female employees face "rampant sexual harassment."

headline: Benzinga's Bulls And Bears Of The Past Week: Nvidia, General Motors, Alibaba, Activision Blizzard And More
summary:
Benzinga has examined the prospects for many investor favorite stocks over the past week. 
	Last week's bullish calls included a top computer chip company and a big three automaker.

headline: Nio Mops Up $2B Following Closure Of At-the-Market Offering; Can Its Beaten Down Stock Find Redemption?
summary: Nio, Inc. (NYSE: NIO) shares fell over 9% in the week ending Nov. 19, underperforming most of its EV peers. The stock, however, saw an upward bounce in late trading on Friday.

headline: Apple Car Rumor Nudges Shares Into Record Territory; What Its Success Means For The Tech Giant
summary: An Apple, Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL) Car could become a  reality  sooner than expected, Bloomberg reported Thursday.

headline: Musk Is Hands-On: After Server Outage Worldwide, Tesla CEO Confirms Tesla App Is Back Online
summary: Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) app experienced a server outage Friday night that left some owners locked out of their cars.
